post.description: This lets you create a checkout session for your customer by generating a Tazapay hosted payment page.

country.description: ISO 3166 standard alpha-2 code. eg: SG, IN, US, etc.

contact_code.description: Country Phone Code (for example, '+91' for India, '+1' for the US)

contact_number.description: Contact number of Individual or Business

seller_id.description: ID of the seller if the checkout session is being created by a marketplace on behalf of a seller. You can get this ID from the response of the Create User API.

reference_id.description: Marketplace identifier value. Example, marketplace escrow number, auto generated value, etc. Will be returned as part of the response body and webhooks.

fee_paid_by.description: The entity that bears the transaction fee - can be 'buyer' or 'seller'

fee_percentage.description: If the fee is to be split between the buyer and seller, pass the percentage split to be paid by the entity passed above. E.g. if fee_paid_by is buyer and fee_percentage is 40%, 60% of the remaining fee will need to be paid by the seller.

invoice_currency.description: Invoice currency, ISO 4217 standard specifies three-letter ("Alpha-3") codes. e.g. USD

txn_description.description: A short description of the underlying transaction or trade to be displayed on the redirection screen

payment_methods.description: Specify payment methods for the transaction. The keyword for each payment method can be found using the collection methods metadata API. Click here to learn more about managing payment methods.

shipping_details.description: Shipping Details for the checkout session

country.description: Address country (Country (ISO 3166-1 alpha_2 country code, in lowercase)

country_code.description: Calling country code (for example, '1' for United States, '91' for India)

billing_details.description: Billing Details for the checkout session

country.description: Address country (Country (ISO 3166-1 alpha_2 country code, in lowercase)

country_code.description: Calling country code (for example, '1' for United States, '91' for India)

callback_url.description: (Backend) URL on your server where Tazapay sends webhooks. Useful for asynchronous payments where we can send updates on payment statuses. Please make sure that the url is under https!

complete_url.description: (Frontend) URL where the customer is redirected after a successful payment. Please make sure that the url is under https!

error_url.description: (Frontend) URL where the customer is redirected in case of an error in the payment. Please make sure that the url is under https!

filter.description: Specify payment methods to remove for the transaction. The keyword for each payment method can be found using the collection methods metadata API. Click here to learn more about managing payment methods.

fee_tier.description: Applicable fee tier. Configured for marketplaces to collect commissions and fees

get.description: This lets you fetch the details of the checkout session

parameters.name: txn_no
parameters.description: Represents a unique transaction. This is available in the response of the Create Checkout session.

contact_code.description: Country Phone Code (for example, '+91' for India, '+1' for the US)

contact_number.description: Contact number of Individual or Business

partners_customer_id.description: Reference number of the user from your system

put.description: Edit or update details of an existing user

account_id.description: Unique ID for a user generated as a part of POST /v1/user response body

contact_number.description: Contact number of Individual or Business

message.example: Unable to update user details at this time, please contact us at ops@tazapay.com and share the following reference ID: <transaction reference>.
something went wrong. please try again later

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: UUID of the Tazapay user received in the response for Create User API.

post.description: Add a bank account for a user

currency.description: Currency of the bank account , ISO 4217 standard three-letter ("Alpha-3") codes. e.g. USD

bank_name.description: Name of the bank, example 'JP Morgan Chase'

legal_name.description: Legal Name of the Account Holder

country_code.description: ISD code of the user's country. Example - '+1' for USA. (Required if contact_number != "" OR Bank country = Malaysia) If this field is set, please make sure to add the country_code for the respective country as mentioned in the example!

contact_number.description: User's mobile number connected to the bank account. (Required if country_code != "" OR Bank country = Malaysia)

address.description: Address of the bank account holder

is_primary_account.description: Set the bank account as primary

city.description: The city where the bank account is located

state.description: The state where the bank account is located

zip_code.description: The zipcode/pincode where the bank account is located

get.description: This returns the list of all the bank accounts added for a user

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: UUID of the user's Tazapay account

put.description: This enables you to set a bank account as primary. The previous bank account becomes non-primary.

account_id.description: UUID of the user's Tazapay account

bank_id.description: UUID of the added bank. This is a part of the response of the Add Bank API

application_type.description: Applicant's entity type. Can be 'Individual' or 'Business'.

entity_name.description: Name of the business/individual (for example - "My Shop"/"John Doe")

submit.description: To ensure the KYB application is picked up for verification, please pass "submit":true. You cannot update the KYB application anymore after 'submit' : 'true' is passed. To keep the option of editing, pass 'submit' : 'false'.

name.description: Name of the business, Example: "My Shop"

type.description: Can be either Sole Proprietorship, Partnership, Company or Other

zip_code.description: Zip code - Part of the address

years_in_business.description: Number of years in which the business is operational. Can be: < 1 year,1-5 years,5-10 years, or> 10 years

date_of_incorporation.description: Date of incorporation of the business

documents.description: List of documents to be used for verification

zip_code.description: Zip Code - Part of the address

ownership_percent.description: Representative's ownership percentage. Value must be between 0-100

roles.description: Representative's role. Can be one or more than one of these: 'Director', 'Shareholder', 'Beneficial Owner', 'Authorised Signatory', 'Authorized Representative', or 'Other'

documents.description: List of documents to be used for verification

proof_type.description: Proof type. Can be Identity, Business, Person, Additional Doc, Address, Business History Doc

type.description: Document type. Can be: Driver's License, National ID, Passport, or Other

name.description: Required only if type = Other. Document type if not listed in "type"

zip_code.description: Zip Code - Part of the address

ownership_percent.description: Representative's ownership percentage. Value must be between 0-100

roles.description: Representative's role. Can be one or more than one of these: 'Director', 'Shareholder', 'Beneficial Owner', 'Authorised Signatory', 'Authorized Representative', or 'Other'

documents.description: List of documents to be used for verification

proof_type.description: Proof type. Can be Identity, Business, Person, Additional Doc, Address, Business History Doc

type.description: Document type. Can be: Driver's License, National ID, Passport, or Other

name.description: Required only if type = Other. Document type if not listed in "type"

callback_url.description: (Backend) URL on your server where Tazapay sends webhooks. Useful for getting the updated status of the KYB Application

put.description: Update existing KYB application. Can be only done if "submit":"false" in the POST KYB request

get.description: This allows you to list the docs required for verifying the KYB of an User based on country.

post.description: Creates an escrow between two parties

txn_type.description: Type of transaction. Can be: `goods`, `service`. If empty; contracted MP level value will get applied

release_mechanism.description: Party responsible for verifying the release documents. Can be: `marketplace`, `tazapay`. If empty; contracted MP level value will get applied

reference_id.description: Marketplace identifier value. Example, marketplace escrow number, auto generated value, etc.

initiated_by.description: Tazapay account UUID of the party who has initiated the transaction

buyer_id.description: Tazapay account UUID of the buyer

seller_id.description: Tazapay account UUID of the seller

txn_description.description: A short description of the underlying transaction or trade to be displayed on the redirection screen

invoice_currency.description: Invoice currency, ISO 4217 standard specifies three-letter ("Alpha-3") codes. e.g. USD

country.description: Address country (Country (ISO 3166-1 alpha_2 country code, in lowercase)

country_code.description: Calling country code (for example, '1' for United States, '91' for India)

country.description: Address country (Country (ISO 3166-1 alpha_2 country code, in lowercase)

country_code.description: Calling country code (for example, '1' for United States, '91' for India)

fee_paid_by.description: Party that bears the fee for the transaction. Can be: `buyer` or `seller`. If empty; contracted MP level value will get applied

fee_percentage.description: If the fee is to be split between the buyer and seller, pass the percentage split to be paid by the entity passed above. E.g. if fee_paid_by is `buyer` and fee_percentage is 40%, 60% of the remaining fee will need to be paid by the seller.

flat_fee.description: Fix fee charged by marketplace in addition to arrange for services such as logistics, inspection, etc. from their end. This is a separate fee from `fee_paid_by` & `fee_percentage`.

label.description: Flat fee description that will be shown to the customer, e.g., ‘Shipping Fee’

paid_by.description: Party that bears the fee for the transaction

fee_percentage.description: If the fee is to be split between the buyer and seller, pass the percentage of split to be paid by the entity passed above. E.g. if fee_paid_by is "buyer" and fee_percentage is 40%, 60% of the remaining fee will need to be paid by the seller

fee_tier.description: Applicable fee tier. If empty, contracted MP level "standard" tier property will be used

release_docs.description: The list of documents to be provided as completion proof for the verification.

type.description: If txn_type is 'goods' then one of 'Commercial Invoice', 'Bill of Lading/ Airway Bill', or 'Others'; if txn_type is 'service' then one of 'Invoice', 'Proof of Service Delivery', or 'Others'

milestone_scheme.description: The scheme code that is set by you. If empty, the transaction is considered as the default scheme of 100% payment and 100% payout

callback_url.description: (Backend) URL on your server where Tazapay sends webhooks. Useful for asynchronous payments where we can send updates on payment statuses.

parameters.name: txn_no
parameters.description: Represents a unique transaction. This is available in the response of the Create Escrow API

post.description: Generates payment links corresponding to an escrow for collection from the buyer

txn_no.description: Represents a unique transaction. Number received in Create Escrow API response

payment_methods.description: Specify payment methods for the transaction. The keyword for each payment method can be found using the collection methods metadata API. Click here to learn more about managing payment methods.

complete_url.description: (Frontend) URL where the customer is redirected after a successful payment

error_url.description: (Frontend) URL where the customer is redirected in case of an error in the payment

callback_url.description: (Backend) URL on your server where Tazapay sends webhooks. Useful for asynchronous payments where we can send updates on payment statuses

filter.description: Specify payment methods which need to be filtered out (REMOVED) for the given transaction.

get.description: Returns the status of an escrow. Know more about escrow states and substates.

post.description: To release funds for a particular transaction to your bank account.

txn_no.description: Represents a unique transaction. This is available in the response of the Create Escrow API.

release_docs.description: Documents for verification before release of payment. A sample json can be found here: https://docs.tazapay.com/reference/release-intro

callback_url.description: URL on your server to which Tazapay sends webhooks to.

post.description: To initiate a refund of an existing paid transaction back to source

txn_no.description: Represents a unique transaction. This is available in the response of the Create Escrow API

amount.description: Amount to be refunded. If omitted, the request will be registered for a refund of the full transaction amount

callback_url.description: URL on your server to which Tazapay sends webhooks to intimating you of the refund status

remarks.description: Field to any additional details concerning the refund request

documents.description: Documents to support the refund request

parameters.name: txn_no
parameters.description: The transaction number against which you want to initiate a refund

parameters.name: reference_id
parameters.description: The reference id that you received in the response of refund request API (https://docs.tazapay.com/reference/refund-api)

oneOf.title: 200 OK - reference_id is passed in the request body
200 OK - if txn_no is passed in the request body

get.description: This API will allow you to check whether the buyer-seller pair is a compatible corridor for transaction creation.

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: ISO 3166 standard alpha-2 code. eg: SG, IN, US, etc.

get.description: This checks whether the invoice currency for the buyer-seller pair is supported.

parameters.name: buyer_country
parameters.description: ISO 3166 standard alpha-2 code. eg: SG, IN, US, etc.

parameters.name: seller_country
parameters.description: ISO 3166 standard alpha-2 code. eg: SG, IN, US

get.description: This allows you to check the collection methods available for a given buyer/seller country. This also shows the supported collection methods for the invoice currency and country corridor, the banks and type of accounts that will be available.

parameters.name: buyer_country
parameters.description: ISO 3166 standard alpha-2 code. eg: SG, IN, US

parameters.name: invoice_currency
parameters.description: Invoice currency, ISO 4217 standard specifies three-letter ("Alpha-3") codes. e.g. USD

parameters.name: seller_country
parameters.description: ISO 3166 standard alpha-2 code. eg: SG, IN, US

parameters.name: seller_id
parameters.description: ID of an account. Useful for fetching information specific to your sub-sellers.

message.example: country is not supported at this moment

get.description: This enables you to get the summary of the disbursement methods available for the provided country corridor. This also provides the FX rate between the invoice currency and settlement currency.

parameters.name: buyer_country
parameters.description: ISO 3166 standard alpha-2 code. eg: SG, IN, US

parameters.name: seller_country
parameters.description: ISO 3166 standard alpha-2 code. eg: SG, IN, US

parameters.name: invoice_currency
parameters.description: Invoice currency, ISO 4217 standard specifies three-letter ("Alpha-3") codes. e.g. USD

parameters.name: seller_id
parameters.description: ID of an account. Useful for fetching information specific to your sub-sellers.

message.example: country is not supported at this moment
